findings.title: connectAll and disconnectAll are near-identical (18 lines each)
findings.description: connectAll() (lines 42-60) and disconnectAll() (lines 62-80) share identical structure -- the only difference is calling entry.channel.connect() vs entry.channel.disconnect(). This is 18 lines duplicated nearly verbatim, exceeding the 10-line near-duplicate threshold.

findings.title: Handler add/remove pattern duplicated 5 times across files
findings.description: The push-handler / return-unsubscribe-closure pattern (push + indexOf + splice) appears 5 times: SlackChannel.onStatusChange (line 53), SlackChannel.onMessage (line 97), WhatsAppChannel.onStatusChange (line 62), WhatsAppChannel.onMessage (line 100), and ChannelRegistry.onAnyMessage (line 82). Each instance is 5-6 lines of identical logic with only the array name changing. Since the pattern appears 5 times (well above the 3+ threshold for extraction), it is a candidate for a shared utility.

findings.title: setupEventHandlers has 4-level nesting depth
findings.description: setupEventHandlers contains 4 levels of nesting: callback body -> for loop -> conditional guards -> inner for loop (dispatching to handlers). Level 1: ev.on callback, Level 2: for...of event.messages, Level 3: guard clauses (if/continue), Level 4: for...of messageHandlers. The 4-level depth hits the P2 threshold and makes the message-parsing logic harder to follow.

findings.title: Slack connect() uses if/else where guard clause is cleaner
findings.description: The connect() method uses if/else to check deps, with the happy path inside the if-block and the throw in else. The WhatsApp channel (line 73) already uses the cleaner guard-clause pattern (if (!this.deps) throw ... then proceed). The Slack version should match for consistency and reduced nesting.

positive_observations: All functions are short (under 30 lines), consistent with project conventions
Types are cleanly separated in types.ts with clear interface definitions
Guard clauses and early returns used well in WhatsApp channel (connect, setupEventHandlers)
Cyclomatic complexity per function stays in the 1-4 range (within project thresholds)
No nested ternaries, no magic numbers, good use of named constants (60_000 for rate limit window)
